steps.title: Identify the contrasting reactions
steps.content: Anne and Marilla respond oppositely to the maple branches: Anne finds them beautiful and exciting (asking “Don't they give you a thrill—several thrills?”); Marilla finds them messy (calling them “Messy things”).

steps.title: Recognize what the contrast reveals
steps.content: The entire scene—Anne's exuberant love of nature versus Marilla's dismissal of it—is designed to show that these two characters have fundamentally different personalities. The other choices either focus narrowly on Anne alone (A), assume an unstated pattern of repeated conflict (B), or isolate only Marilla's reaction (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
